About.Name: Dat Thai
About.AboutText:
Hi, I am Dat Thai. I am a passionate electrical engineering student with a keen interest in circuit. Currently, I am pursuing my undergraduate degree at Seattle Pacific University. I am dedicated to expanding my knowledge and skills in various areas of electrical engineering.

I enjoy tackling challenging programming projects and I thrive in collaborative environments where I can contribute my expertise and learn from my peers.

B

Project1.Title: Peak Detector Circuit
Project1.Desc:
Designed and built a peak detector circuit using op-amps, a diode, and a capacitor to detect the peak voltage of an input signal.

The circuit uses an LM339 comparator to compare the peak detector output against a reference voltage, triggering an LED indicator when the signal exceeds the threshold. Tested and debugged at frequencies ranging from 10kHz to 25kHz.
